name: Quick Fresh-Pack Dill Pickles
recipeInstructions:
Yield: 7 to 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash cucumbers.
2. Cut a 1/16-inch slice off the blossom end and discard, but leave 1/4-inch of stem attached.
3. Dissolve 1 1/4 cups salt in 2 gallons water.
4. Pour over cucumbers and let stand 12 hours.
5. Drain.
6. Combine vinegar, 1/2 cup salt, sugar, and 2 quarts water. Add mixed pickling spices tied in a clean white cloth.
7. Heat to boiling.
8. Fill jars with cucumbers.
9. Add 1 teaspoon mustard seed and 1-1/2 heads fresh dill per pint.
10. Cover with boiling pickling solution, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
11. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or use the low-temperature pasteurization treatment (for more information see "Low-Temperature Pasteurization Treatment").
